mechanism_narrative: Federal funds rate declines below 0.5% signal an ultra-loose monetary regime that reduces equity discount rates and increases asset price multiples. This mechanism operates continuously and predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when FEDFUNDS is in the lower tail of its historical distribution.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when FEDFUNDS < 0.5% do not exceed mean returns when FEDFUNDS > 2.0% by at least 150 basis points (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate exceeds 9.0% and remains elevated, signaling labor market slack and reduced consumer spending power. This threshold-triggered condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward returns in periods when UNRATE > 9.0% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline), or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) and retail sales (RSAFS) jointly signal aggregate demand strength. When both series show positive momentum over the prior quarter, equity investors increase growth expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days. The mechanism requires both signals to align; either series alone is insufficient.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 growth > 0% and RSAFS growth > 0% over prior 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1=13260.7B and RSAFS=373103M indicate demand activity
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.04 over any 3-year rolling window, or 60-day forward returns when both GDPC1 and RSAFS are positive do not exceed baseline by at least 200 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines more than 2% month-over-month, signaling a contraction in manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side weakness. This threshold-triggered condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as investors reprrice earnings expectations downward.
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 2% are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline), or Rank IC < -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts (HOUST) fall below 600 thousand units annualized, indicating a contraction in residential construction demand and forward-looking economic weakness. This threshold-triggered mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the housing sector is a leading indicator of broader economic activity.
evidence_refs: fact:current HOUST=610.0 thousand units is near threshold
regime_hint: housing market slowdown or demand contraction
failure_condition: Mean 20-day forward returns when HOUST < 600K do not differ significantly from baseline (t-test p > 0.05), or Rank IC > -0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50 thousand jobs per month, signaling labor market momentum loss and reduced consumer income growth. This threshold-triggered condition predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days as weak labor markets foreshadow demand weakness.
regime_hint: labor market deceleration or recession onset
failure_condition: Mean 60-day forward returns in periods of weak PAYEMS growth (< 50K/month) are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline), or Rank IC < -0.04 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.0% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 1.0%, creating a negative real interest rate regime. This interaction-driven mechanism signals monetary policy accommodation that supports equity valuations, predicting positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days. The signal requires both conditions to hold simultaneously.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL=218.372 and FEDFUNDS=0.19% indicate potential negative real rates
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with inflation backdrop
failure_condition: Rank IC of the interaction signal < 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns when both conditions hold do not exceed baseline by at least 100 basis points (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP (GDPC1) contracts quarter-over-quarter (negative growth), signaling economic recession. This always-on mechanism predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days as equity investors immediately reprice risk and growth expectations downward upon recession confirmation.
failure_condition: Mean 5-day forward returns in quarters with negative GDPC1 growth are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05 vs. baseline), or Rank IC > -0.05 over any 2-year rolling window.
